title: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of vibrant Lebanese Red Lentil Soup (Adas) garnished with fresh cilantro and a lemon wedge.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a comforting bowl of homemade Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, a staple of Middle Eastern cuisine.

description: Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, known as Adas, is a heartwarming and nutritious staple in Middle Eastern cuisine. This vibrant, wholesome soup is incredibly easy to prepare, blending earthy red lentils with aromatic vegetables and a bright squeeze of lemon. Perfect for a comforting meal on its own or as a delicious appetizer, it is a truly satisfying and healthy dish.

instructions.title: Prepare Lentils and Vegetables
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Set aside. Prepare your vegetables by finely chopping the onion, dicing the carrots, and mincing the garlic. This preparation ensures a smooth cooking process.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and diced carrots, sautéing for about 5-7 minutes until the onion is translucent and the carrots begin to soften. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Spices
instructions.text: Add the rinsed lentils to the pot along with the cumin powder and turmeric powder. Stir well to coat the lentils and vegetables with the spices, cooking for 1-2 minutes to toast the spices and enhance their flavor.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth (or water), salt, and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are completely tender and have broken down, creating a thick, creamy consistency.

instructions.title: Finish with Lemon
instructions.text: Once the lentils are cooked, remove the pot from the heat. Stir in the fresh lemon juice. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed, adding more salt, pepper, or lemon juice to your preference. If you prefer a smoother soup, you can use an immersion blender to blend a portion or all of the soup to your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Serve Adas Hot
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas into bowls. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve immediately with warm pita bread or toasted bread on the side for dipping. A drizzle of extra virgin olive oil can also be added for richness.

tips: For extra depth of flavor, bloom your spices in the hot oil for 30 seconds before adding liquids. This helps release their aromatic compounds.
Adjust the consistency of your soup by adding more hot broth if it is too thick. If you like it thicker, simmer uncovered for a few extra minutes.
Store leftover Adas in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. It often tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld.
article:
Discover the heartwarming embrace of Middle Eastern cuisine with a bowl of Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, affectionately known as Adas. This iconic soup is more than just a dish; it's a staple, a comfort, and a testament to the rich, vibrant flavors of the region. Bursting with nourishing ingredients and simple yet profound spices, Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is a culinary journey that promises both satiety and satisfaction. Whether you’re seeking a wholesome meal on a chilly evening, a light lunch, or an introduction to the wonders of Lebanese cooking, this timeless recipe delivers warmth, health, and an explosion of taste in every spoonful. Its humble ingredients transform into something truly extraordinary, making it a beloved favorite across generations.

## What is Lebanese Red Lentil Soup Adas?

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, or Adas b'hamod (lentils with lemon), is a quintessential dish in Lebanese and broader Middle Eastern culinary traditions. It’s celebrated for its simplicity, nutritional value, and incredibly comforting flavor profile. At its core, Adas is a thick, creamy soup made primarily from red lentils, which cook down quickly and provide a wonderfully smooth texture. Unlike some other lentil soups, the Lebanese version often features a bright, tangy finish from generous amounts of fresh lemon juice, complemented by the refreshing aroma of dried or fresh mint.

The soup’s base typically consists of sautéed onions, carrots, and sometimes celery, providing a sweet and savory foundation. Cumin is the star spice, lending its earthy warmth, often accompanied by turmeric for a beautiful golden hue and additional health benefits. Adas is naturally vegan and gluten-free, making it an accessible and beloved option for many dietary preferences. It’s a testament to how humble ingredients can be transformed into a dish that is both deeply satisfying and incredibly healthy, packed with protein, fiber, and essential nutrients. This makes Lebanese Red Lentil Soup not just a delicious meal, but a truly wholesome one.

## Ingredients You Need

To create this authentic and flavorful Lebanese Red Lentil Soup, gather these fresh and pantry staples:

*   **Red Lentils:** 1.5 cups, rinsed thoroughly. These cook quickly and break down easily, creating a creamy texture.
*   **Olive Oil:** 3 tablespoons, for sautéing and drizzling. Use good quality extra virgin olive oil for best flavor.
*   **Onion:** 1 large, finely chopped.
*   **Carrots:** 2 medium, peeled and finely chopped.
*   **Garlic:** 4-5 cloves, minced.
*   **Tomato Paste:** 1 tablespoon (optional, but adds depth and color).
*   **Cumin:** 1.5 teaspoons, ground. This is the signature spice.
*   **Turmeric:** 0.5 teaspoon, ground (optional, for color and subtle earthy notes).
*   **Vegetable Broth or Water:** 6-7 cups. Broth adds more flavor, but water works just as well.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To taste.
*   **Fresh Lemon Juice:** 1/4 to 1/2 cup, freshly squeezed, added at the end. Essential for the authentic tangy flavor.
*   **Fresh Mint:** 1/4 cup, finely chopped, for garnish.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Creating this comforting Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is a straightforward process. Follow these steps for a perfect bowl:

1.  **Prepare Lentils:** Rinse the red lentils under cold water in a fine-mesh sieve until the water runs clear. Set aside.
2.  **Sauté Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and carrots. Sauté for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables soften and the onion becomes translucent.
3.  **Add Garlic and Spices:** Add the minced garlic and, if using, the tomato paste, cumin, and turmeric to the pot. Cook for another 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until fragrant. This helps to toast the spices and deepen their flavor.
4.  **Combine Lentils and Liquid:** Stir in the rinsed lentils into the pot. Pour in 6 cups of vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil over medium-high heat.
5.  **Simmer and Cook:** Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down, and the soup has thickened. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. If the soup becomes too thick, add the remaining cup of broth or water until you reach your desired consistency.
6.  **Blend (Optional):** For an extra smooth and creamy soup, you can use an immersion blender to partially or fully blend the soup directly in the pot. Alternatively, carefully transfer portions of the soup to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot. Be cautious when blending hot liquids.
7.  **Finish with Lemon:** Remove the pot from the heat. Stir in the fresh lemon juice. Start with 1/4 cup and add more to taste, depending on how tangy you like your soup. Season generously with salt and black pepper.
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Lebanese Red Lentil Soup into bowls. Garnish each serving with a sprinkle of fresh chopped mint and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil. Serve immediately.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, authentic taste for your Lebanese Red Lentil Soup involves a few key details:

*   **Rinse Your Lentils Thoroughly:** This step is crucial. Rinsing removes any debris and helps prevent the soup from becoming cloudy or foamy.
*   **Don't Rush the Sauté:** Allowing the onions and carrots to soften and slightly caramelize before adding other ingredients builds a foundational layer of flavor that will make your soup incredibly rich.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Red lentils absorb a lot of liquid. Keep an eye on the soup as it simmers and don't hesitate to add more hot broth or water if it becomes too thick for your liking. Some prefer it very thick, others a bit thinner.
*   **Fresh Lemon is Non-Negotiable:** The bright, tangy finish from fresh lemon juice is what truly defines Lebanese Red Lentil Soup. Add it at the very end to preserve its vibrant flavor. Avoid bottled lemon juice if possible.
*   **Fresh Mint Garnish:** While dried mint can be used in a pinch, fresh mint offers a burst of aromatic freshness that elevates the soup significantly. Add it just before serving.
*   **Taste and Adjust:** Always taste your soup before serving and adjust salt, pepper, and lemon juice as needed. Flavors can vary, so trust your palate.

## Serving Suggestions

Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is wonderfully versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways:

*   **Traditional:** Serve it piping hot with wedges of fresh lemon on the side, allowing each person to add more tang to their liking. A basket of warm pita bread or toasted pita chips is essential for scooping up every delicious drop.
*   **Light Meal:** It makes a satisfying and complete light lunch or dinner on its own, especially when paired with a simple side salad like a crisp garden salad or a vibrant tabbouleh or fattoush for added freshness.
*   **Appetizer:** Offer smaller portions as a starter to a larger Middle Eastern feast, paving the way for dishes like kibbeh, shish tawook, or falafel.
*   **Warmth in Winter:** This soup is particularly comforting during colder months, providing a nutritious and soul-warming meal that is both hearty and healthy.
*   **Extra Drizzle:** Don't forget the final flourish of extra virgin olive oil just before serving; it adds a silken texture and enhances the overall richness.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is perfect as is, you can certainly experiment with variations to suit your taste or available ingredients:

*   **Vegetable Boost:** Feel free to add other finely diced vegetables such as celery, potatoes (cubed), spinach (stirred in at the end until wilted), or even a handful of chopped Swiss chard for extra nutrients and texture.
*   **Spice It Up:** For a touch of heat, a pinch of cayenne pepper or red pepper flakes can be added along with the other spices. A dash of ground coriander can also complement the cumin beautifully.
*   **Different Lentils:** While red lentils are traditional and ideal for their quick cooking and creamy texture, you could use brown or green lentils if preferred. Just note that they will require longer cooking times and may not break down as much, resulting in a chunkier soup.
*   **Garnish Options:** Beyond fresh mint, consider garnishing with toasted pita croutons, a sprinkle of sumac for extra tang, or crispy fried onions for added texture and flavor. If you're not vegan, a dollop of plain yogurt or a swirl of sour cream can add a creamy richness.
*   **Herb Swaps:** While mint is classic, fresh parsley or cilantro can also be used as a garnish, offering a different aromatic profile. A mix of herbs can also be delightful.

This Lebanese Red Lentil Soup is a testament to the power of simple, wholesome ingredients to create a deeply satisfying and nourishing meal. It’s a dish that warms the body and soul, embodying the hospitality and rich culinary heritage of the Middle East. Whether you’re a seasoned cook or new to Middle Eastern cuisine, this recipe is approachable, rewarding, and guaranteed to become a cherished favorite in your kitchen. Enjoy the comforting embrace of this timeless soup!
